title: The diagram below shows how instant noodles are manufactured.
prompt: The diagram below shows how instant noodles are manufactured. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

paragraphs.text: The diagram below shows how instant noodles are manufactured.
paragraphs.italic_text: The diagram below shows how instant noodles are manufactured.

paragraphs.text: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
paragraphs.italic_text: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

paragraphs.text: The diagram illustrates the entire process of manufacturing instant noodles, from the original flour to the packaged instant noodles ready for sale.
Overall, eight steps are contained in the entire procedure of making instant noodles, which can be divided into noodles production and packaging.
Overall, the process consists of eight stages, which can be divided into noodle production and packaging.
paragraphs.italic_text: Overall, the process consists of eight stages, which can be divided into noodle production and packaging.

paragraphs.text: The machines mix the stored flour with water and oil, slice the dough and produce noodles discs.
After the discs are cooked with oil and dried, the packaging machine will put the noodles and other ingredients into the cups and labelled them.
After the discs are fried in oil and dried, they are placed into cups with other ingredients and then labelled and sealed.
paragraphs.italic_text: After the discs are fried in oil and dried, they are placed into cups with other ingredients and then labelled and sealed.

paragraphs.text: To begin with the noodles production stage, the flour is trucked to the storage silos and stored there before being put into the mixer together with water and oil.
At the beginning of the noodle production stage, flour is transported to storage silos before being transferred to a mixer, where water and oil are added.
paragraphs.italic_text: At the beginning of the noodle production stage, flour is transported to storage silos before being transferred to a mixer, where water and oil are added.

paragraphs.text: The dough produced in this step is then made into sheets with rollers.
In the next two processes, dough sheets are sliced into strips and further shaped into noodles discs.
The noodles discs are fried and dried afterwards.
In terms of packaging, the noodles cakes are put into cups with spices and vegetables.
These cups are labelled and sealed subsequently and finally made into instant noodles for retail.
The cups are then sealed and labelled, ready for distribution and sale.
paragraphs.italic_text: The cups are then sealed and labelled, ready for distribution and sale.

paragraphs.text: Good attempt at grouping (production vs packaging)
The diagram illustrates the process of manufacturing instant noodles, from the initial storage of flour to the final packaged product ready for sale.

Overall, the production involves eight stages, which can be broadly divided into two phases: noodle production and packaging. Flour is first processed into dough, which is then shaped into noodle discs, before being fried and dried. In the final stages, the dried noodle cakes are packed into cups with additional ingredients and prepared for distribution.

At the beginning of the process, flour is delivered to storage silos and subsequently transferred to a mixer, where it is combined with water and oil. The resulting dough is then passed through a series of rollers to form thin sheets. These sheets are cut into strips and shaped into circular noodle discs. Following this, the discs are fried in oil and then dried to remove excess moisture.

In the packaging stage, the dried noodle cakes are placed into individual cups, to which vegetables and spices are added. Finally, the filled cups are sealed and labelled, producing finished instant noodle products ready for sale.(182)

Mention number of steps or grouping
Overall, the process consists of [number] stages, beginning with [first stage] and ending with [final stage].It can be divided into [main phases], such as [phase 1] and [phase 2].
Describe the first half of the process
At the first stage, [input] is [processed].Next, it is [action], before being [next action].Following this, [result].
Continue the process to the end
In the subsequent stages, [process continues].After that, [step].Finally, [final product/result].
